title: Aggregate stats double-count matches from BR-Football-Dataset in unfiltered queries
evidence: src/dataLoader.ts:48 BRFootballMatch has no `competition`/`season` fields; src/matchQueries.ts:334 getAllMatches includes brFootball rows, which overlap real-world matches already present in Brasileirao/Libertadores CSVs. Unfiltered getAverageGoals/getHeadToHead therefore count the same match twice.
suggestion: Tag brFootball rows with a competition/season and exclude or de-duplicate them from unfiltered aggregate queries, mirroring the Brasileirao-Historical de-dup already applied.

title: BR-Football matches silently excluded from all season-filtered queries
evidence: src/matchQueries.ts:322 getMatchSeason returns 0 for brFootball (no `season` field parsed in src/dataLoader.ts:173), so any filter.season causes these ~10k matches to be dropped without notice.
suggestion: Parse a year from BRFootballMatch.date into a season field so season filters are consistent across datasets.

title: Team-name alias table covers only 3 full-name variants
evidence: src/teamNormalizer.ts:1 ALIASES only maps Corinthians and São Paulo full names; the spec's Data Quality Notes list full names like 'Sport Club Corinthians Paulista' as a general case. Other full-name/abbreviation variants won't match across datasets.
suggestion: Broaden alias coverage or add fuzzy matching for full club names; add tests for a wider set of variants.

title: getTopScoringTeams and getPlayersByClub implemented but not exposed as MCP tools
evidence: src/teamQueries.ts:543 getTopScoringTeams and src/playerQueries.ts:585 getPlayersByClub exist and are tested but are not registered in the 8-tool list in src/server.ts:867.
suggestion: Register these as MCP tools (e.g. get_top_scoring_teams, get_players_by_club) to surface capabilities the spec's example answers reference (per-club Brazilian player counts).
